description_text:
AI Engineer (entry level, multiple openings) 💰 Salary: $110,000 - 150,000 per year

At Uptimecrew we are looking for a ML, AI engineer!

🛠️ Our tech stack:
AI, Backend, CI/CD, Cloud, DevSecOps, REST, Security, Web, Machine-Learning

📝 Rquirements:
- Practical coding experience through studies, projects, or internships
- Solid understanding of data structures, algorithms, and fundamental web or backend concepts
- Quick learner with the ability to thrive in an Agile environment
- Bachelors degree or equivalent qualification
- Legally permitted to work in the United States without company sponsorship
- Willingness to work on-site at one of our specified locations

👩‍💻👨‍💻 Your responsibilities are:
- Promote the integration of AI-driven DevSecOps practices using large language models and automation tools to enhance the secure containerization of legacy applications while ensuring security compliance
- Design and oversee AI-augmented CI/CD pipelines that automatically produce security policies, container setups, and infrastructure-as-code models based on application needs and industry standards
- Utilize AI coding tools for development, refactoring, and documentation in line with our team protocols
- Create unit and integration tests to boost coverage and minimize defects
- Expedite pull requests with AI-supported reviews, summaries, and risk assessments
- Analyze logs and documentation using AI to resolve issues and facilitate workflow
- Collaborate with product, design, security, and compliance teams to deliver increments that adhere to fintech regulations

View this job and over 500 other transparent jobs with salaries (💰💰💰) & tech stacks (🛠️) on DevITJobs

Category: ML, AI Developer / Engineer
Location address: Church Street 850, San-Bernardino, United States

Salary: $110,000 - 150,000 per year

Benefits & perks that we offer:

Uptimecrew - More about us and the role:
At Uptime Crew, we are a collective of engineers and technicians dedicated to maintaining environments where precision, discipline, and reliability are paramount. Our operations occur within real, continuously active production systems at leading global tech firms, where safety is our utmost priority, consistency is crucial, and uptime is a philosophy. We offer an annual salary range of $110,000 to $150,000, comprehensive health insurance covering medical, vision, and dental needs, an Employee Stock Purchase Plan (ESPP), matching 401(k), generous paid time off including 14 paid holidays, a week off at year-end, birthday leave, tuition reimbursement, and a range of additional benefits. We invite you to become part of our team that is committed to ensuring the future remains online.

Are you looking for ML, AI jobs in San-Bernardino?

Why choose between doing meaningful work and having a fulfilling life? At MITRE, you can have both. That's because MITRE people are committed to tackling our nation's toughest challenges—and we're committed to the long-term well-being of our employees. MITRE is different from most technology companies. We are a not-for-profit corporation chartered to work for the public interest, with no commercial conflicts to influence what we do. The R&D centers we operate for the government create lasting impact in fields as diverse as cybersecurity, healthcare, aviation, defense, and enterprise transformation. We're making a difference every day—working for a safer, healthier, and more secure nation and world. Our workplace reflects our values. We offer competitive benefits, exceptional professional development opportunities for career growth, and a culture of innovation that embraces adaptability, collaboration, technical excellence, and people in partnership. If this sounds like the choice you want to make, then choose MITRE - and make a difference with us.

NOTE:  Thank you for your interest in MITRE’s Early Careers opportunities. Please be aware that this is not an application for a specific position. By submitting your information and providing your resume, you will be included in a pool of candidates for various roles across the company. If you are selected for consideration for a particular position a member of MITRE’s Early Careers recruiting team will reach out to you.  You can monitor your application status here: Workday (myworkdayjobs.com).  

Job Description

Data-driven, intelligent systems can bring immense computing power to bear, revealing insights across datasets too massive for humans to synthesize. We help our customers to build and enable those systems by creating trusted data engineering architectures, machine learning algorithms, and analytic techniques that put the power of big data at our users' fingertips while leverage deep expertise in human decision-making and collaboration to best pair humans and machines.

As a recent graduate working in data science, operations research, math and statistics, you will work with a team to apply your knowledge and gain experience with one or more of the following: 
• Data analysis and visualization
• Augmented decision making and trusted analytics
• AI architecture, strategy and policy
• AI-enhanced decision making and insights
• Generative AI and large language models
• Machine learning operations
• Applied math, statistics and optimization
• Simulation and computational analytics
• Systems modeling and analysis
• Operational effectiveness and efficiency
• Knowledge discovery
• Next-gen data environments

Basic Qualification
• Must have a Bachelor’s, Master’s or PhD degree in Data Science, Artificial Intelligence, Math, Statistics or a related degree/program
• Knowledge of how retrieve data from databases and other systems and tools using queries, exporting capabilities, and other effective methods
• Understanding of or experience with statistical software and data visualization tools such as R, SPSS, SAS, and UNIX shell scripting
• Sustained excellence in academic performance

Preferred Qualifications
• Experience having applied data science, artificial intelligence, math, statistics or a related learning to an internship or research/lab position.
• Experience retrieving data from databases and other systems and tools using queries, exporting capabilities, and other effective methods
• Experience with statistical software and data visualization tools such as R, SPSS, SAS, and UNIX shell scripting
• Proficiency in one or more of the following: Python (scikit-learn, NumPy, pandas, Jupyter), Scala/Java, C/C++/C#, Objective C; database design & development (SQL, Neo4J, MongoDB); AWS
• Demonstrated interest in serving the public

Clearance: In addition to passing MITRE’s employment background check, you may be submitted for a U.S. Government Security clearance.  This is determined by the Department of Defense’s adjudicative guidelines for receiving a clearance, to include U.S. citizenship. Other jobs may require vetting for various levels of agency public trust, fitness, suitability, controlled access, and special programs access determination consistent with the work program to which you are assigned.

This requisition requires the candidate to have a minimum of the following clearance(s):

This requisition requires the hired candidate to have or obtain, within one year from the date of hire, the following clearance(s):

Salary compensation range and midpoint:
$80,000 - $100,000 - $120,000 Annual

Work Location Type:
Hybrid

It is unlawful in Massachusetts to require or administer a lie detector test as a condition of employment or continued employment. An employer who violates this law shall be subject to criminal penalties and civil liability.

Commitment to Non-Discrimination

All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to disability, status as a protected veteran or any other status protected by applicable federal, state, local or international law.

MITRE intends to maintain a website that is fully accessible to all individuals. If you are unable to search or apply for jobs and would like to request a reasonable accommodation for any part of MITRE’s employment process, please email recruitinghelp@mitre.org for general support and collegerecruiting@mitre.org for intern positions. This service is for individuals requiring reasonable accommodation requests. Please note that vendor solicitations will not receive a reply.

Benefits information may be found here.

Job Description

MITRE leverages deep technical knowledge, engineering expertise, and prototyping capabilities to develop, evaluate, and test creative and practical solutions that facilitate tech transition and inform acquisition. In our electronic systems and engineering role we innovative and practical solutions in the areas of sensing, communications, processing, and more, and works across MITRE to define and deliver technical products to meet critical mission challenges. 

As a recent graduate working in electrical or computer engineering, you will work with a team to apply your knowledge and gain experience with one or more of the following: 
• Electronic warfare
• Antennas and electromagnetics
• Sensors and radars
• Photonics
• Signal processing 
• Communications, SIGINT, spectrum, and position, navigation, and timing (PNT) 
• Electronics and embedded security 
• Rapid electronic and hardware prototyping, advanced manufacturing, design, mechanical and reliability analysis
• Maritime System Development, acoustic & ocean modeling, SONAR system analysis, and transducer design
• Machine-sensing and perception

Basic Qualifications
• Must have a Bachelor’s, Master’s or PhD in Electrical Engineering, Computer Engineering or a related degree program
• Knowledge of electrical engineering theory and one or more of the following: digital signal processing, wireless communications, computer architecture, embedded software, embedded security, cryptography, analog design, RF/EM, sensors and antennas, photonics, power systems, and software architecture and design
• Sustained excellence in academic performance

 Preferred Qualifications
• Experience having applied electrical engineering or computer engineering learning to an internship or research/lab position
• "Hands-on" skills in at least one or more language
• Demonstrated interest in serving the public

Clearance: In addition to passing MITRE’s employment background check, you may be submitted for a U.S. Government Security clearance.  This is determined by the Department of Defense’s adjudicative guidelines for receiving a clearance, to include U.S. citizenship. Other jobs may require vetting for various levels of agency public trust, fitness, suitability, controlled access, and special programs access determination consistent with the work program to which you are assigned.

This requisition requires the candidate to have a minimum of the following clearance(s):

This requisition requires the hired candidate to have or obtain, within one year from the date of hire, the following clearance(s):

Salary compensation range and midpoint:
$80,400 - $100,500 - $120,600 Annual

Work Location Type:
Hybrid

Position Overview

Important: GM does not provide immigration-related sponsorship for this role. Do not apply if you will need GM immigration sponsorship now or in the future (e.g., H1-B, OPT, STEM OPT, CPT, TN, J-1, etc.). We are seeking a high-performing Mechanical Design Engineer to lead the in-house development of GM electronics design. You will be responsible for the mechanical design, development, and review of Electrical Controllers for our next generation SDV architecture. This is a Hybrid role, requiring you to report to a Metro Detroit location at least three times per week, while building strong partnerships with internal teams and external suppliers.

Key Responsibilities
• Execute mechanical design of ECUs per program timing, leading to successful product launch
• Own module HW (and in some cases SW) release
• Design mechanical components including castings, injection moldings, stampings, seals, electrical interface, PCBA mounts, connector interface, and fastening
• Perform design calculations, GD&T and tolerance stacks
• Create 3D CAD and 2D CAD documentation and lead CAD reviews with stakeholders
• Develop analysis and test plans, perform proof of concept tests and analysis
• Lead prototyping and testing, analyze test data, and adjust mechanical designs as needed
• Collaborate with electrical engineers and PCBA designers to integrate components into electro-mechanical systems
• Maintain validation documentation and tracking
• Assess and aid in selection of semi-conductors for assigned modules
• Build partnerships with internal functional peers and Tier 1 suppliers to align on ECU design per development cycles
• Develop virtualization technologies to accelerate and de-risk product development
• Communicate design progress and risks to stakeholders and customers
• Drive the mechanical design strategy for the enterprise

Required Qualifications
• Pursuit of a Bachelor’s degree in Mechanical Engineering or a related field
• Expected graduation between December 2025 and June 2026
• Availability to work full-time (40 hours per week)

Preferred Qualifications
• Proficiency in 3D CAD modeling (NX and Teamcenter preferred) and 2D drawing creation
• Strong analytical skills and understanding of solid mechanics, material science, heat transfer, mechanism design, and manufacturing principles
• Hands-on experience engaging with electronics manufacturers
• Experience in the mechanical design of PCBAs, computers, or automotive electronics
• Understanding of thermal solutions and their application
• Knowledge of vibrational and structural analysis
• Familiarity with ASME Y14.5-2009 dimensioning and tolerancing standards

Compensation
• Base Salary: Determined based on factors relevant to the position and the candidate’s geography
• Bonus Potential: An incentive pay program offers payouts based on company performance, job level, and individual performance

Benefits & Perks
• GM offers a variety of health and wellbeing benefit programs including medical, dental, and vision plans
• Benefit options also include a Health Savings Account, Flexible Spending Accounts, and a retirement savings plan
• Additional perks include sickness and accident benefits, life insurance, paid vacation & holidays, tuition assistance programs, an employee assistance program, GM vehicle discounts, and more

Job Description

You don’t see it, but it’s there. Our employees work on the world’s most advanced electronics – from saving emissions in the City of Lights to powering the Mars Rover to protecting the F-35 fighter jet. At Electronic Systems, you’ll be among the brightest minds, working on the aerospace and defense industry’s most difficult problems. Drawing strength from our differences, we’re innovating for the future. And you can, too.

Our flexible work environment provides you a chance to change the world without giving up your personal life. We put our customers first – exemplified by our missions: “We Protect Those Who Protect Us®” and “We Innovate For Those Who Move The WorldTM.”

Sound like a team you want to be a part of? An exciting future at BAE Systems awaits and it starts with Year One— a unique experience to jumpstart your engineering career.

We are looking for entry level candidates to join our ES Defense Hardware Engineering Team here at BAE Systems, Inc. BAE Systems is seeking entry level mechanical engineers to fill a variety of mechanical engineering positions based on your background, skill set and interest level. Beyond exciting work, each hire in engineering will be part of our Year One program.

In this exciting role, you will enjoy opportunities to take on challenging work assignments, flexible scheduling with every other Friday off, an inviting and supportive culture, competitive compensation, and an excellent benefits package that includes tuition reimbursement for advanced degrees.

As part of the program, you explore different projects and technologies by participating in rotations. You also develop skills by receiving immediate mentorship from experienced managers and engineers, and participate in classes and workshops to expand your knowledge of yourself, technology and our company. Finally, you network and collaborate to make lasting connections with peers, co-workers and leadership.

Mechanical Engineering Job Description
1. Support Product Design. Provide Mechanical Engineering support to the design, development and selection of basic hardware components utilizing fundamental mechanical engineering principles, theories and techniques. Assigned duties may include design and development of specific packaging modules, mechanisms or components to meet specified design requirements. May receive direction from senior engineering staff, and also may be responsible to provide direction to drafting.
2. Perform Mechanical Analyses. Obtain working knowledge of company standard analysis tools. Perform basic static, dynamic and heat transfer analyses of mechanical structures. Compile data for investigation by senior mechanical engineering personnel.
3. Support Verification/Validation of Design. Support the fabrication and environmental testing of design hardware. Assist in the design and fabrication of prototype hardware. Support the environmental testing of hardware including: vibration, shock, thermal, fatigue and others as required. Document results for review by senior engineering personnel.
4. Prepare Documentation. Generate and review the technical content of drawings, engineering specifications, reports and other documentation associated with the mechanical design. Generate and present design review packages for internal and external audiences.
5. Support Continuous Improvement. Acquire a working knowledge of ES Quality System and the Mechanical Design Process. Acquire up-to-date knowledge of developments in mechanical engineering disciplines.
6. Support to Sustainment. Provide Mechanical Engineering support to products in production. Manage material issues, perform root cause assessments of discovered issues. perform analysis as required to verify validity of proposed solutions.
• * Please note: Candidates for these positions will be considered for positions across the greater Nashua, NH area and various engineering hiring teams. **

Because this role involves a combination of collaborative/in-person and independent work, it will take the form of a hybrid work format, with time split between working onsite and remotely.

Required Education, Experience, & Skills • This position is open to applicants with varying levels of experience. To be considered for an Mechanical Engineer I, you must have at least a Bachelor’s degree. To be considered for a Mechanical Engineer II, you must have at least a Master’s degree.
• Strong Mechanical Aptitude
•Ability to support design and development of hardware to ensure requirements
compliance
•Ability to read mechanical drawings and perform tolerance assessments
•Ability to perform basic thermal / dynamic analysis to ensure good design

Preferred Education, Experience, & Skills •Good communication skills and organizational skills

Pay Information
Full-Time Salary Range: $63653 - $108210

Please note: This range is based on our market pay structures. However, individual salaries are determined by a variety of factors including, but not limited to: business considerations, local market conditions, and internal equity, as well as candidate qualifications, such as skills, education, and experience.

Employee Benefits: At BAE Systems, we support our employees in all aspects of their life, including their health and financial well-being. Regular employees scheduled to work 20+ hours per week are offered: health, dental, and vision insurance; health savings accounts; a 401(k) savings plan; disability coverage; and life and accident insurance. We also have an employee assistance program, a legal plan, and other perks including discounts on things like home, auto, and pet insurance. Our leave programs include paid time off, paid holidays, as well as other types of leave, including paid parental, military, bereavement, and any applicable federal and state sick leave. Employees may participate in the company recognition program to receive monetary or non-monetary recognition awards. Other incentives may be available based on position level and/or job specifics.
About BAE Systems Electronic Systems BAE Systems, Inc. is the U.S. subsidiary of BAE Systems plc, an international defense, aerospace and security company which delivers a full range of products and services for air, land and naval forces, as well as advanced electronics, security, information technology solutions and customer support services. Improving the future and protecting lives is an ambitious mission, but it’s what we do at BAE Systems. Working here means using your passion and ingenuity where it counts – defending national security with breakthrough technology, superior products, and intelligence solutions. As you develop the latest technology and defend national security, you will continually hone your skills on a team—making a big impact on a global scale. At BAE Systems, you’ll find a rewarding career that truly makes a difference. Electronic Systems (ES) is the global innovator behind BAE Systems’ game-changing defense and commercial electronics. Exploiting every electron, we push the limits of what is possible, giving our customers the edge and our employees opportunities to change the world. Our products and capabilities can be found everywhere – from the depths of the ocean to the far reaches of space. At our core are more than 14,000 highly talented Electronic Systems employees with the brightest minds in the industry, we make an impact – for our customers and the communities we serve.

This position will be posted for at least 5 calendar days. The posting will remain active until the position is filled, or a qualified pool of candidates is identified.

Position Overview

We are hiring a Machine Learning Engineer to help design, build, and deploy ML solutions. This role focuses on turning models into reliable systems and collaborating with engineering teams to operationalize ML.

Key Responsibilities
• Develop and train ML models with reproducible pipelines
• Build data preprocessing workflows and feature generation
• Deploy models to production and monitor performance
• Work with stakeholders to define success metrics and test outcomes
• Document experiments and contribute to best practices

Required Qualifications
• Degree in CS, Data Science, Engineering, or related field
• Strong Python, data handling (pandas), and ML fundamentals
• Familiarity with scikit-learn and basic model evaluation
• Understanding of software engineering basics (Git, testing, modular code)

Preferred Qualifications
• PyTorch or TensorFlow
• Experience with ML pipelines, Airflow, or workflow orchestration
• Exposure to CI/CD, Docker, and cloud services

Position Overview

A global technology company is seeking a Developer to innovate on systems driving the quantum revolution and AI technology. The ideal candidate will have a background in Electrical or Computer Engineering, possess strong communication skills, and be passionate about technology.

Key Responsibilities
• Responsibilities: Designing solutions for clients and experimenting with forward-thinking ideas.

Required Qualifications
• Qualifications: Background in Electrical or Computer Engineering.
• Qualifications: Strong communication skills.
• Qualifications: Passion for technology.

Benefits & Perks
• Benefits: Collaborative environment where imagination and curiosity drive outcomes.

## Bring more to life with Leica Biosystems

Leica Biosystems, part of the Danaher family of companies, is focused on advancing cancer diagnostics and improving lives. We design diagnostic solutions that help clinicians deliver timely, accurate answers. You will join a diverse, collaborative global community that values your unique viewpoint and uses continuous improvement to turn ideas into impact.

This Hardware Engineering Intern role is based in Vista, CA and is on-site as part of the Digital Pathology Business Unit. You will be a member of the Hardware Engineering team and report to the Manager, Hardware Engineering. The position is involved in the design and release of a series of sustainment and value-added value engineering (VAVE) projects for cost reduction, reliability improvement or other general sustainment projects related to Leica Biosystem’s product line of Aperio Whole Slide Imaging Scanners.

### What you will do day-to-day

You will perform system-level testing to produce data for use within design of experiments and work with the engineering team on a variety of hands-on tasks. These tasks include, but are not limited to, machining, electronics, programming, verification & validation, and system-level design and troubleshooting. Under the guidance of senior engineers, you may update or refine engineering drawings or processes.

Your work will support sustainment and VAVE efforts focused on cost reduction and reliability improvement for Aperio Whole Slide Imaging Scanners, contributing directly to products that impact patient care.

### Who we’re looking for

- Currently pursuing a bachelor’s degree in Electrical Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, System Engineering, Biomedical Engineering or similar.

- Strong verbal and written communication skills, with the ability to work independently and in a team environment.

It would be a plus if you also possess previous experience in:

- Some experience with CAD technologies, like Solid. Works, Auto. CAD, or similar.

- Experience working with complex electromechanical systems.

- Extreme comfort with typical Windows programs (Word, Power. Point, Excel, etc.). Bonus points for familiarity with Linux / Unix computer environments.

### Compensation and additional details

The salary range OR the hourly range for this role is $25 - $35. This is the range that we in good faith believe is the range of possible compensation for this role at the time of this posting. This range may be modified in the future.

Leica Biosystems and Danaher are committed to equal opportunity regardless of race, color, national origin, religion, sex, age, marital status, disability, veteran status, sexual orientation, gender identity, or other characteristics protected by law. We comply with federal and state disability laws and make reasonable accommodations for applicants and employees with disabilities. If reasonable accommodation is needed to participate in the job application or interview process, to perform essential job functions, and/or to receive other benefits and privileges of employment, please contact: 1-202-419-7762 or applyassistance@danaher.com.

For more information about Danaher, visit www.danaher.com. Please read our Applicant Data Privacy Notice carefully.

About Monte Carlo

As businesses increasingly rely on data + AI for competitive advantage, reliability has become a non-negotiable. Named a CBInsights AI100 company and described by Forbes as the "New Relic for data", Monte Carlo supports some of the world's most prestigious companies, including Fox, Roche, Honeywell, and CreditKarma to deliver trustworthy data + AI at scale.

Backed by Accel, Redpoint Ventures, Notable Capital, ICONIQ Growth, and Salesforce Ventures, Monte Carlo is powering the future of reliable data + AI.

About the Team

This small, high-impact analytics team moves fast and uses its technical and business skills to help the business understand user behavior. They do it in an engineered way that scales well as the company continually grows in complexity. The team is highly autonomous, collaborative, fun, and deeply competent—building a stand-out analytics function inside one of the most exciting startups in the data space.

You’ll work primarily with US-based Product Managers, but also partner with Technical Account Managers, Marketing and Field Engineering, and any other team that needs someone to bring technical data skills to bear on a business problem.

About the Role

We’re hiring a Data Analyst who is curious to use SQL and user analytics to uncover insights that guide product decisions and build dashboards that support the day-to-day processes of the business. Product development moves quickly at Monte Carlo, so your work will have an immediate and visible impact on what gets built.

This is a generalist analytics role. Some days you’ll focus on producing insights, other days you’ll build dashboards in Looker, and often you’ll create or update dbt models. You’ll serve as the analytics partner for specific product squads—becoming the expert in their domain, their users, and the data that powers decision-making. The team is highly self-sufficient and continuously expanding its skills into new areas.

What You’ll Do
• Product partnership and analytics ownership: Become the aligned analytics partner for specific product squads; develop deep expertise in what can be tracked, how users behave, and which datasets matter; translate business hypotheses into rigorous analytics and clear, actionable insights
• Dashboards and reporting: Build intuitive dashboards and trackers in Looker, Mixpanel, and Fullstory; create easy-to-digest visualizations that Product, TAM, and business teams rely on daily
• Data modeling and data quality: Build and maintain dbt models that aggregate core product usage data; improve data usability across Product, TAM, and GTM teams; ensure data remains trustworthy using Monte Carlo
• Data expertise and best practices: Develop a deep understanding of Monte Carlo’s data, how it is collected, and how it should be interpreted; partner cross-functionally to steward best practices in analytics and product measurement

You’re a Fit If You Have
• 1–3 years of experience as a Data Analyst or Product Analyst
• Demonstrated ability to turn business questions into actionable analyses
• Strong SQL skills and analytical problem-solving ability
• Familiarity with at least one modern BI tool (Looker preferred but not required)
• An undergraduate degree (or equivalent experience) emphasizing analytical thinking

Nice to Have
• Hands-on experience with dbt
• Experience analyzing user or product behavior
• Hands-on knowledge of the modern data stack

#LI-REMOTE

#BI-REMOTE

Come As You Are

Equality is a core tenet of Monte Carlo's culture. We are committed to building an inclusive global team that represents a variety of backgrounds, perspectives, beliefs, and experiences.

Monte Carlo is an equal-opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees.

We are proud to be recognized for our world-class employee experience:

Monte Carlo Named 2025 Databricks Data Governance Partner of the Year

We were recently recognized as the #1 Data Observability Platform by G2 for the 4th consecutive quarter. See our G2 reviews here!

Monte Carlo was featured on Database Trends and Applications (DBTA’s) Trend-Setting Products for 2025!

We are super proud to be named the 2026 Best Place to Work by Built In!

Beware of Imposter Recruiters and Job Scams
• All official communication from our recruiting team will come from an @montecarlodata.com email address.
• We will never ask candidates to provide sensitive personal information (such as bank details, social security numbers, or payment) at any stage of the recruitment process.
• We will never request payment for equipment, training, or application processing.
• Our open positions are always listed on our official careers page: https://jobs.ashbyhq.com/montecarlodata.

If you are contacted by someone claiming to represent Monte Carlo but you’re unsure of their legitimacy, please reach out to us directly at recruiting@montecarlodata.com before sharing any personal information.

Compensation:
$100K – $125K • Offers Equity

Position Overview

Fresher Data Analyst positions available in Location: Baltimore. Entry-level opportunities for recent graduates. Additionally, we are hiring cybersecurity and biotech professionals in Baltimore. Apply through Cosmoquick.

Description

Acquire up-to-date knowledge of developments in mechanical engineering disciplines. 6. Support to Sustainment. Provide Mechanical Engineering support to products in production. Manage material issues, perform root cause assessments of discovered issues. perform analysis as required to verify validity of proposed solutions. Please note: Candidates for these positions will be considered for positions across the greater Nashua, NH area and various engineering hiring teams. Because this role involves a combination of collaborative/in-person and independent work, it will take the form of a hybrid work format, with time split between working onsite and remotely. Required Education, Experience, & Skills • This position is open to applicants with varying levels of experience. To be considered for an Mechanical Engineer I, you must have at least a Bachelor's degree. To be considered for a Mechanical Engineer II, you must have at least a Master's degree. • Strong Mechanical Aptitude • Ability to support design and development of hardware to ensure requirements compliance • Ability to read mechanical drawings and perform tolerance assessments • Ability to perform basic thermal / dynamic analysis to ensure good design Preferred Education, Experience, & Skills • Good communication skills and organizational skills Pay Information Full-Time Salary Range: $63653 - $108210 Please note: This range is based on our market pay structures. However, individual salaries are determined by a variety of factors including, but not limited to: business considerations, local market conditions, and internal equity, as well as candidate qualifications, such as skills, education, and experience. Employee Benefits: At BAE Systems, we support our employees in all aspects of their life, including their health and financial well-being. Regular employees scheduled to work 20 hours per week are offered: health, dental, and vision insurance; health savings accounts; a 401(k) savings plan; disability coverage; and life and accident insurance. We also have an employee assistance program, a legal plan, and other perks including discounts on things like home, auto, and pet insurance. Our leave programs include paid time off, paid holidays, as well as other types of leave, including paid parental, military, bereavement, and any applicable federal and state sick leave. Employees may participate in the company recognition program to receive monetary or non-monetary recognition awards. Other incentives may be available based on position level and/or job specifics. About BAE Systems Electronic Systems BAE Systems, Inc. is the U.S. subsidiary of BAE Systems plc, an international defense, aerospace and security company which delivers a full range of products and services for air, land and naval forces, as well as advanced electronics, security, information technology solutions and customer support services. Improving the future and protecting lives is an ambitious mission, but it's what we do at BAE Systems. Working here means using your passion and ingenuity where it counts - defending national security with breakthrough technology, superior products, and intelligence solutions. As you develop the latest technology and defend national security, you will continually hone your skills on a team-making a big impact on a global scale. At BAE Systems, you'll find a rewarding career that truly makes a difference. Electronic Systems (ES) is the global innovator behind BAE Systems' game-changing defense and commercial electronics. Exploiting every electron, we push the limits of what is possible, giving our customers the edge and our employees opportunities to change the world. Our products and capabilities can be found everywhere - from the depths of the ocean to the far reaches of space. At our core are more than 14,000 highly talented Electronic Systems employees with the brightest minds in the industry, we make an impact - for our customers and the communities we serve. Vega is hiring! Fulltime job opportunity, Entry level BI Analyst. Must be located in New Jersey or live within a commutable distance to Montvale, NJ

The Business Intelligence Analyst (Entry Level) supports the Business Intelligence & Data team by understanding and writing data queries, building visualizations, and working with end users to obtain clear and concise report requirements. The candidate should have familiarity with data models/relationships and strong analytical skills with great attention to detail. Eagerness to learn the business is a must.

Key Responsibilities
• Assist in developing, maintaining, and enhancing dashboards and reports using BI tools
• Extract, transform, and load (ETL) data from various sources into BI tools and databases
• Perform data validation, reconciliation, and quality checks across multiple data sources
• Translate business requirements into technical reporting specifications
• Document data sources, transformations, metrics definitions, and report logic
• Collaborate with BI & Data team and business stakeholders to produce impactful reports
• Foundational knowledge of SQL (joins, aggregations)
• Experience working with structured datasets (tables, schemas, relationships)
• Familiarity with basic data warehousing and BI concepts
• Detail-oriented
• Ability to multi-task
• Strong communication skills with both technical and nontechnical users
• Bachelor s degree in Business Intelligence, Computer Science, Data Analytics, Information Systems, or a related field
• Hands-on experience with BI tools (experience with Qlik products is a plus)
• Familiarity with SQL Server
• Data Modeling Fundamentals
